column1: Please Restore Your Account Access,"New Page 1 Dear customers: Wells Fargo is constantly working to increase security for all Online Banking users. To ensure the integrity of our online payment system, we periodically review accounts. Your account might be place on restricted status. Restricted accounts continue to receive payments, but they are limited in their ability to send or withdraw funds. To lift up this restriction, you need to login into your account (with your username or SSN and your password), then you have to complete our verification process. You must confirm your credit card details and your billing information as well. All restricted accounts have their billing information unconfirmed, meaning that you may no longer send money from your account until you have updated your billing information on file. To initiate the billing update confirmation process, please follow the link bellow and fill in the necessary fields: Thank you, Wells Fargo - Online Banking About Wells Fargo | Employment | Report Email Fraud | Privacy, Security & Legal | Home © 1995 - 2005 Wells Fargo. All rights reserved.",1,1

Re: Suggestion for Mercury Language Integration with Cloud Management Deployment,"Dear Isiah,
Thank you for reaching out and sharing your valuable suggestion! We appreciate you taking the time to provide such detailed feedback.

We understand how integrating Mercury language features with our Cloud Management platform could significantly enhance your development and deployment workflows. The ability to manage infrastructure and deployments directly from your Mercury codebase aligns with our goal of providing a seamless and efficient development experience.
We have forwarded your suggestion to our product team for further evaluation. While we cannot promise immediate implementation, we will certainly consider it for future development plans.
In the meantime, please let us know if you have any other questions or suggestions. We are always happy to hear from you.

Informasjon til deg som er tillitsvalgt i Pensjonistforbundet Takk for den gode jobben du gjør!

Vi er nå litt over tre aktive måneder inn i 2025, og kan ta en velfortjent pust i bakken. Jeg vil gjerne takke hver især for den viktig og gode jobben dere gjør, rundt om i hele landet.Pensjonistforbundet jobber for aldersvennlige lokalsamfunn, der alle kan leve gode liv. Vi lager sosiale møteplasser for å motvirke ensomhet. Vi jobber politisk, for å sikre økonomisk trygghet og velferd for alle, og helsetjenester når vi trenger det. Pluss mye mer.

La det ikke være noen tvil: Sammen gjør vi samfunnet bedre! Og når vi tar fatt igjen etter påskedagene, må vi jobbe videre for å invitere flere inn i fellesskapet vårt, slik at vi får enda bedre uttelling for den gode jobben vi gjør. Det er vårt felles mål.Med ønske om en god påske,Jan Davidsenforbundsleder
Tips til aktivitet: Bocciaturnering for ung og gammel!
Pensjonistforbundet Hemsedal inviterer unge og gamle til bocciaturnering hver 2. mandag i måneden. Boccia er en sport for alle, uansett alder eller erfaring, og denne aktiviteten skaper engasjement!🎉

TikTok v. Montana,"Forbes NewslettersPlus: U.S. Workers Are Using Weed At Record Levels, But Are They High On The Job? ADVERTISEMENTBECOME A MEMBER • Newsletters • MyForbesGood morning, As someone who drives a dated (arguably ancient) car, I know all too well the frustration that comes with recall notices—Carfax says there have been around 200 “Do Not Drive” or “Park Outside” vehicle recalls in the past two decades. Still, it was shocking to read that an estimated 2.5 million models with a serious recall are still unrepaired. California, Texas and Florida lead the nation with over 200,000 recalled vehicles each. Carfax maintains a complete list of recalled vehicles and affected model years here, and prospective buyers can also check used cars that could be at risk here.Drive safe out there, Sarah Whitmire Senior Editor, Newsletters Follow me on Twitter and Forbes.comBREAKING NEWSAP/Alex Brandon President Joe Biden and House Speaker Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif.) both said their meeting last night to discuss raising the debt ceiling was “productive,” though it’s still not clear how the two leaders will find common ground. Meanwhile, Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen reiterated in a letter to Speaker McCarthy Monday that it is “highly likely” the government could run out of money to pay off its bills in 10 days.E. Jean Carroll asked a federal court to award new damages after former President Donald Trump denied sexually assaulting her at a CNN town hall earlier this month. “It makes a mockery of the jury verdict and our justice system if he can just keep on repeating the same defamatory statements over and over again,” Carroll’s attorney said. BUSINESS + FINANCEAP/Lawrence Neumeister, FileCharlie Javice, founder of defunct student loan assistance company Frank, pleaded not guilty to fraud charges for allegedly lying about her company’s database when she sold it to JPMorgan Chase in 2021 for $175 million. Javice was arrested last month on charges from the DOJ and SEC—and is also facing a separate lawsuit from JPMorgan. Analysts at Bank of America have high hopes for the S&P 500 this year, raising its year-end price target for the index by 8% on the optimism of companies’ recent cost-cutting and the AI boom in tech stocks. That’d be the S&P’s highest level since August, but only 2% higher than where the index landed yesterday. WEALTH + ENTREPRENEURSHIPThe dollar is weak, the world is hungry and the Russians are making a mess of the grain markets. Farmer-Financier Sal Gilbertie’s food-based inflation wedge could add some spice to your portfolio, when conditions are right. His Teucrium Trading offers pure-play exchange-traded funds that hold futures positions in corn, wheat, soybeans and sugar. TECH + INNOVATIONTo the surprise of likely no one, TikTok sued the state of Montana for its ban signed into law last week that would take effect next year. The suit alleges Montana’s new law suppresses free speech allowed under the First Amendment and seeks to have the ban overturned, permanently prevented from implementation and declared unconstitutional.Cryptocurrency platform Gemini Exchange planned to file a $1 billion claim against Genesis, putting pressure on Digital Currency Group after it missed a $630 million payment last week. Genesis filed for bankruptcy at the start of 2023 in the wake of the FTX collapse, and DCG owes its crypto lender unit over $1.65 billion. SPORTS + ENTERTAINMENTGetty Images10-time NBA all-star Carmelo Anthony announced he would retire from basketball Monday after 19 seasons in the league. The 38-year-old retires as the No. 9 scorer in league history, and having made more than $260 million in playing salary and bonuses during his career. More: Could LeBron be next? He hinted as much in a postgame news conference last night.TRAVEL + LIFESTYLEThe NAACP issued a formal travel advisory for Florida: “Under the leadership of Governor DeSantis, the state of Florida has become hostile to Black Americans,” president and CEO Derrick Johnson wrote. It follows a similar travel warning from the League of United Latin American Citizens last week. As recently as March, the state was setting tourism records, and Florida welcomed a record 137.6 million visitors last year. Daily Cover Story Getty ImagesA Record Number Of American Workers Are Using Cannabis—That Doesn’t Mean They’re High On The JobRead ArticleTOPLINEThe latest findings from a key drug screening company might lead you to believe U.S. workers need an intervention—a record number of American workers tested positive for marijuana use last year. Overall, Quest Diagnostics found marijuana positivity increased by 10.3% in just one year, from 2021 to 2022.But as any occasional toker who’s had to pass a drug test knows, that data does not mean workers are actually getting high on the job. People who consume marijuana, and critically, the plant’s psychoactive component THC, can test positive for weeks after exposure. It’s because THC is lipophilic, meaning it is stored in body fat and can stick around for a while. For this reason, urine tests cannot determine if a person is high at the moment of the test. “The problem is they don't have a test for impairment,” says Dr. Peter Grinspoon, a primary care physician at Massachusetts General Hospital. “If you have a positive urine test for cannabis…it has no implication that you've been impaired before work or even within the last 72 hours.”Currently, 22 states and Washington, D.C., have legalized recreational use for adults 21 years and older, while 38 states have legal medical marijuana programs. Even though marijuana is still federally illegal, many employers have stopped or minimized screening for weed, including Amazon, and even the FBI and CIA have relaxed their rules around drug testing.WHY IT MATTERSLegalization critics like Kevin Sabet believe the Quest Diagnostics report is a bad omen: “I think it's worrisome that we're seeing this, especially at our workplaces and in safety sensitive positions, putting other people at risk…The issue is whether we think it's okay to be a stoned society or not.” Dr. Grinspoon sees this viewpoint as “based on moral panic, not the science.” He suggests that ‘zero tolerance’ laws, often based on limited drug screening capability, are “more about persecuting cannabis users than promoting public safety.” MOREWhy Cannabis Jobs Are Going Up In SmokeADVERTISEMENTFACTS AND COMMENTSAs Covid-19 vaccination rates among children continue to lag, a round of recent studies confirmed previous findings that vaccines are effective in children and pregnant people: 32.9%Share of U.S. children ages 5 to 11 who received a Covid vaccine as of early May 5+ millionEstimated number of missed school days in children ages 5 to 17 that could’ve been averted with more widespread adoption of Covid booster vaccines No associationResearchers found no link between Covid vaccines given to pregnant people and spontaneous abortionsSTRATEGY AND SUCCESSIt’s a standard way for employers to wrap up a job interview, but it can still stop even the most promising candidates in their tracks: “Do you have any questions for me?” We’ve got a few suggestions for questions to ask at the end of your next interview, including: Is this a new position? If not, why did the previous employee leave? VIDEOJeffrey Epstein Threatened To Expose Bill Gates’ Affair With Russian Bridge Player, Report SaysWatch nowGAMESQUIZThree Southwestern U.S. states struck a deal to reduce their water usage in an attempt to preserve the Colorado River amid historic drought and overuse. Which state was not part of the agreement?A.CaliforniaB.UtahC.NevadaD.ArizonaCheck Your AnswerFRASE BY FORBESUnscramble today’s anagram to solve this hidden phrase!Scroll to the bottom of today’s newsletter for a hint.Play Now TODAY'S PHRASE ACROSS THE NEWSROOMAnnouncing the 2023 Forbes 30 Under 30 Local Lists. We’re looking for the top young founders and leaders in the innovation hubs of Atlanta, Austin, Boston, Chicago, Cleveland, Los Angeles, Miami, Puerto Rico, Seattle and Toronto. The deadline to submit nominations is June 9.BEYOND THE NEWSROOMThe 2023 Forbes Future Of Work Summit will take place in New York and virtually on June 1. We’ll explore the forthcoming impact of artificial intelligence, shifting dynamics between workers and employers, and more. Sign up to stay tuned for updates.Thanks for reading! Follow us on Twitter for by-the-minute updates on the latest business and financial news throughout the day.
